blocks.text: With the real protocol now on the table, here's exactly how a2uicatalog.ai sits relative to it — including a correction to language used elsewhere in this codebase.

blocks.title: A correction, stated plainly
blocks.text: a2uicatalog's own emitter code describes itself as covering "conformance tiers 1+2" with "standard-mappable" atoms. Those are a2uicatalog's own terms — not language the real A2UI spec uses anywhere. The real, load-bearing mechanism underneath that framing is genuine spec vocabulary: the Basic Catalog vs. Custom Catalog system, and catalogId-scoped extension components (module 3). The tiering is a useful internal way to talk about it; it just isn't Google's terminology, and shouldn't be presented as such.
The real numbers, checked live against the schema

a2uicatalog ships 468 stable atoms. Of those, 19 have a direct mapping to one of the 18 real Basic Catalog components (module 3's table) — things like `heading` -> `Text`, `progress_bar` -> a styled `Column`. The remaining 449 travel as **extension components** under a2uicatalog's own catalogId: valid, correct A2UI behavior — a host without that catalog simply skips them, which the spec treats as expected, not an error.

blocks.caption: a2uicatalog's atom split (live count)
headers: What happens on a bare A2UI host
rows: Maps to a Basic Catalog component
Renders only if the host loads a2uicatalog's catalog

blocks.text: The blocks dialect is a convenience, not the protocol

Every payload in this brief has used a2uicatalog's own authoring shape: `{title, theme, blocks: [...]}`, blocks keyed by `type`. It's simpler to hand-write than the real adjacency-list format, and it's what this catalogue's atom pages, playgrounds, and demos all use directly. The `a2ui-emit` tool is the bridge: it walks that dialect and produces a genuine, spec-conformant `createSurface` message — auto-minting deterministic component IDs, building the flat adjacency list, mapping the 19 standard-mappable atoms to their real Basic Catalog components, and passing everything else through as extension components under a2uicatalog's catalogId.
Curated content and the graduation pipeline

Atoms have a lifecycle independent of the protocol question above. **`stage: preview`** atoms live in the repo only — no published page, no catalog entry, sometimes deliberately no typed fields at all. **Stable** atoms get an atom page, catalog entries, schema validation, contract tests — and CI enforces that every published artifact matches the stable set exactly.

blocks.title: A worked example — the rocket
blocks.text: gdm_rocket_panel shipped as hand-curated, field-less preview content inside this same MCP Apps surface. Once proven, it graduated: typed fields (side, layer, loop), a published page, catalog entries. A fireworks animation now holds the preview slot it came from. Curated first, schema when proven — that doorway is the catalog's intake pipeline, and it's a2uicatalog's own design choice, layered on top of (not defined by) the real protocol.

blocks.question: Are "conformance tiers 1+2" and "standard-mappable" terms used in Google's A2UI spec?
options: Yes, they're defined in the v1.0 catalog rules
No — they're a2uicatalog's own framing, built on the spec's real Basic/Custom-catalog and catalogId mechanism
Only in the v0.9 spec, removed in v1.0
Yes, but only in the MCP transport binding docs
blocks.explanation: This is the correction this module opened with. The underlying mechanism (Basic Catalog, Custom Catalog, catalogId-scoped extensions) is real and load-bearing spec vocabulary; the tier/standard-mappable labels on top of it are a2uicatalog's own.

points: a2uicatalog is an independent catalog + renderer on top of A2UI, not the protocol itself
"Conformance tiers" / "standard-mappable" are a2uicatalog's own terms for the real Basic/Custom-catalog mechanism, not Google's
19 of 468 stable atoms map to real Basic Catalog components; the rest are valid extension components under a2uicatalog's catalogId
The blocks dialect is an authoring convenience; a2ui-emit converts it into genuine, spec-conformant A2UI v1.0 messages
preview -> stable is a2uicatalog's own tested pipeline, orthogonal to A2UI protocol conformance

blocks.text: That's the brief. You now know the real protocol — its message envelope, component model, data binding, actions, transports, and its actual relationship to MCP Apps — plus exactly where a2uicatalog sits relative to all of it.
